concept: Maintain / Keep in Condition / Sustain Functioning
description: To keep something in good working condition through ongoing attention and care. 'Whiranu' names the practice of upkeep—the regular attention that keeps tools, relationships, systems, and bodies functioning well over time.
sound_symbolism: The word sustains through gentle persistence. 'Whi' /ʍi/ begins with breathy attention, awareness directed. Then 'ra' reaches with rolling effort, care applied. Finally 'nu' grounds with nasal stability, condition upheld.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mi lo themo whiranu' (I maintain something). Can specify what: 'mi lo nesi whiranu' (I maintain family relationships), 'mi lo kulema whiranu' (I maintain the garden). Related to nelore (preserve) but emphasizes active upkeep rather than protection from change.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Maintenance culture values repair and upkeep over replacement—caring for what we have rather than discarding and replacing.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional crafts require ongoing maintenance—tools, buildings, and textiles last generations through regular care.
